prompt: A 19th-century naturalist scientific illustration of a mountainous terrain, rendered in fine ink and watercolor style. A small explorer descends step-by-step along the steepest downward path of the terrain, following natural slopes toward a valley. The landscape is detailed with geological textures, subtle shading, and earthy tones such as sepia, ochre, and muted greens. The composition resembles an observational scientific plate from the 1800s, with careful attention to linework and realism. No text, no labels, no diagrams, no modern elements, no symbols.
tradition_approach: Fine ink linework, muted watercolor palette, observational realism, geological detailing, and scientific plate composition to reflect naturalist illustration grammar rather than modern rendering.
